name: Pinares de Rodeno and Laguna de Bezas Loop
name_original: Campamento de los maquis y Laguna de Bezas — circular desde la Balsa del Pinar por los Pinares de Rodeno
description: This moderate 26.2-mile mountain bike route explores the unique red sandstone landscapes and pine forests of Pinares de Rodeno.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Rubiales offers diverse terrain, from singletrack near Aras de los Olmos to mixed trails and access roads near Albarracín. The region is characterized by Rodeno pine forests, juniper groves, and oak woodlands, with notable natural features like the Balsa del Pinar wetland and the Cabriel River. Riders can explore varied landscapes, including the Sierra de Albarracín and river paths, providing a range of challenges for mountain bikers. The area features both downhill-focused trails and longer routes, including sections of the GR 10. Best mountain bike trails around Rubiales The most popular mountain bike trail is Pinares de Rodeno and Laguna de Bezas Loop, a 26.2 miles (42.1 km) trail that takes 4 hours 0 minutes to complete. This moderate route features significant elevation gain through scenic pine forests and past the Laguna de Bezas. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Pico Javalón and Ermita de San Cristóbal Loop, a moderate 18.2 miles (29.4 km) path. This route includes climbs to Pico Javalón and passes the historic Ermita de San Cristóbal, offering panoramic views. Local mountain bikers also love the Balsa del Pinar de Rubiales from El Campillo, a 12.3 miles (19.8 km) trail leading through pine forests to the Balsa del Pinar, often completed in about 1 hour 38 minutes. Mountain biking around Rubiales is defined by pine forests, river valleys, and varied elevation changes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging climbs. The routes in Rubiales are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 9 reviews. More than 130 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Rubiales's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Rubiales?
A: The terrain around Rubiales is diverse, featuring Rodeno pine forests, juniper groves, and oak woodlands. You'll find a mix of singletrack, especially near Aras de los Olmos in Valencia, and mixed trails with access roads, particularly closer to Albarracín in Teruel. The region includes varied landscapes, from river valleys to the Sierra de Albarracín, offering a range of surfaces and elevation changes.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Rubiales?
A: Yes, there are trails suitable for easier rides. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004982958/balsa-del-pinar-de-rubiales-from-el-campillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balsa del Pinar de Rubiales from El Campillo&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 12.3-mile (19.8 km) route that leads through pine forests to the Balsa del Pinar, making it a good option for those seeking a less challenging experience.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Rubiales?
A: Rubiales is rich in natural beauty. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4483018" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balsa del Pinar&lt;/a&gt;, a significant wetland surrounded by Rodeno pine. The region also features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/756481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Bezas&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5357686" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arquillo de San Blas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. Near Albarracín, you might encounter the Herrería Waterfall and the 'Ojos del Cabriel' natural springs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5128467" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalaviar Nature Trail&lt;/a&gt; is another notable attraction.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the Rubiales area?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005007204/pinares-de-rodeno-and-laguna-de-bezas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pinares de Rodeno and Laguna de Bezas Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 26.2-mile (42.1 km) trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1003616464/pico-javalon-and-ermita-de-san-cristobal-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Javalón and Ermita de San Cristóbal Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views over 18.2 miles (29.4 km).

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Rubiales?
A: Given the diverse terrain and potential for snowmelt affecting wetlands like Balsa del Pinar, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking in Rubiales. These seasons provide milder temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer can be hot, especially in inland areas like Teruel, while winter might bring colder temperatures and snow at higher elevations.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails available?
A: The trails around Rubiales cater to various skill levels. You'll find easy loops, moderate routes with significant elevation gain like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005007204/pinares-de-rodeno-and-laguna-de-bezas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pinares de Rodeno and Laguna de Bezas Loop&lt;/a&gt;, and more challenging climbs. There are 6 moderate and 5 difficult routes among the total 12 available, ensuring options for experienced riders as well.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes or connections in Rubiales?
A: Yes, the Los Rubiales area near Albarracín, Teruel, is notable for being part of the GR 10 Valencia Portugal route. This long-distance trail connects the Atlantic with the Mediterranean, offering opportunities to link into longer rides from the region.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Rubiales?
A: The mountain bike routes in Rubiales are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 9 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, scenic pine forests, and the options available for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any trails suitable for advanced mountain bikers?
A: Yes, Rubiales offers challenging routes for advanced riders. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38152193" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabrerizo Viewpoint – Albarracín loop from Dornaque&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 44.3-mile (71.3 km) route with significant elevation changes, providing a demanding experience for experienced mountain bikers.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking trails in Rubiales?
A: While specific parking locations are not detailed in the route descriptions, areas like El Campillo, which serves as a starting point for routes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004982958/balsa-del-pinar-de-rubiales-from-el-campillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balsa del Pinar de Rubiales from El Campillo&lt;/a&gt;, typically have designated parking facilities for trail access. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: Are there any cafes or pubs near the mountain bike trails in Rubiales?
A: The region around Rubiales, particularly near towns like Albarracín or Aras de los Olmos, will likely have local cafes and pubs where you can refuel after your ride. While specific establishments are not listed, these towns are known for their amenities catering to visitors.
tips: A historic place in the Sierra de Albarracín that surely will not disappoint us. We find the enclave of the maquis camp during the Spanish Civil War. The Maquis were guerrilla groups that, at the end of the Spanish Civil War, took to the mountains to survive and this camp was one of the most important. If you want to know a little more about its interesting history, do not hesitate to visit it.

The Bezas lagoon is not only a place where you can stop to breathe fresh air, but also symbolizes the largest lagoon in the Sierra de Albarracín.

It is located and forms part of the protected landscape of the Pinares de Rodeno. Depending on the season you are in, it will have more or less water.
A very particular wetland near the town of Rubiales. An obligatory stop on the Empty Mountains route or if you are in the area touring the beautiful landscapes and trails.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Campamento de los maquis y Laguna de Bezas mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route is circular and starts from the Balsa del Pinar. You can typically find parking facilities near this starting point, which is a common access point for trails in the Pinares de Rodeno area.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Komoot classifies this as a moderate mountain bike ride. While much of it is on forest roads and double tracks, there are some short, more technical single-track sections and challenging unpaved segments. It requires good physical fitness and some cycling experience. Beginners might find certain parts demanding, and you may need to push your bike at times.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Campamento de los maquis y Laguna de Bezas route?
faqs.answer: The terrain primarily consists of forest roads and double tracks through extensive pine forests, particularly the rodeno pine. You'll encounter striking red sandstone rock formations, ravines, and crystal-clear water pools. There are also some short, more technical single-track sections. The landscape is characterized by dramatic red rock outcrops and ancient junipers and holm oaks.

faqs.question: What notable features or landmarks will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: This route takes you through the unique Rodeno School Camp (Campamento de los Maquis), a historically significant hiding place for republican guerrillas. You'll also pass by the beautiful Laguna de Bezas, one of the few wetlands in the Sierra de Albarracín, and the Balsa del Pinar. The entire route is within the Protected Landscape of Pinares de Rodeno, known for its distinctive red sandstone formations and prehistoric rock art sites.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best seasons for mountain biking this route are spring, summer, and autumn. These seasons generally offer pleasant weather conditions for enjoying the trail and its natural beauty. Be prepared for potential heat during summer months and carry sufficient water.

faqs.question: Is the Campamento de los maquis y Laguna de Bezas route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Pinares de Rodeno is a natural area, it's always best to keep dogs on a leash, especially given the presence of wildlife such as deer, foxes, and squirrels. As it's a protected landscape, respecting local regulations regarding pets is important to preserve the environment and ensure the safety of your dog and local fauna.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to mountain bike in the Pinares de Rodeno Protected Landscape?
faqs.answer: The Pinares de Rodeno is a Protected Landscape, recognized for its natural and cultural value. While it holds a "Q for Tourist Quality" certification, there is no mention of specific permits or entrance fees required for general mountain biking access to the area in the available information. However, always respect local signage and regulations.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known mountain bike paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other official trails. You'll ride parts of the Camino al Collado de los Burros, MontañasVacías, Rubiales - Orihuela del Tremedal, Casa Forestal de Ligros - Campamento de los Maquis, Pista de los Serranos, and Pista de la Mina, among others.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife and plants can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The tranquil environment of the Pinares de Rodeno increases your chances of spotting wildlife such as deer, foxes, and squirrels. The area is rich in Mediterranean flora, including ancient junipers, holm oaks, and the characteristic rodeno pine with its reddish bark.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near the Balsa del Pinar or Laguna de Bezas?
faqs.answer: The area around Balsa del Pinar and Laguna de Bezas is quite natural and remote. While specific amenities directly at the trailhead might be limited, the nearby towns in the Sierra de Albarracín region, such as Bezas or Albarracín itself, would offer cafes, pubs, and various accommodation options for visitors.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty, length (over 42 km), and elevation gain, it's advisable to bring sufficient water, especially on warmer days. Essential mountain biking gear, a repair kit, snacks, and appropriate clothing for the season are also recommended. A GPS device or the komoot app is useful for navigation.
